stories.key_takeaway: Meta's massive $21 billion expansion of its CoreWeave agreement signals the tech giant's aggressive commitment to securing long-term AI computing resources while boosting CoreWeave's customer diversification away from its heavy Microsoft dependence.

stories.headline: Perplexity ARR Tops $450 Million as Agent Pivot Drives 50% Monthly Growth
stories.key_takeaway: Perplexity's $450 million ARR milestone marks a decisive strategic pivot from AI search challenger to autonomous agent platform, with usage-based pricing unlocking explosive growth that exceeds even internal targets for the full year.
stories.article_excerpt: Perplexity has shattered revenue records with a stunning 50% revenue jump in March 2026, pushing annual recurring revenue past $450 million. The milestone, first reported by the Financial Times, represents an addition of approximately $145 million in annualized revenue in just a single month and marks a crucial inflection point as the startup pivots from AI search engine to autonomous agent platform powered by its new Computer product.

stories.headline: Revolut Launches AIR AI Financial Assistant to 13 Million UK Customers
stories.key_takeaway: Revolut has launched AIR, an AI-powered in-app financial assistant to 13 million UK customers, marking a major step in making financial management conversational and accessible through advanced AI technology.
stories.article_excerpt: Revolut launches AIR, an AI financial assistant, to 13 million UK customers on April 9, 2026, enabling conversational banking with zero data retention.

stories.headline: Intel and Google Cloud Expand Multiyear AI Chip Partnership with Xeon and Custom IPUs
stories.key_takeaway: Intel and Google have formalized a multiyear partnership combining traditional CPU computing with custom infrastructure processors, signaling that specialized hardware diversity—not GPU dominance alone—is critical for efficient, cost-effective AI infrastructure at scale.
stories.article_excerpt: Intel and Google announced a landmark expansion of their strategic partnership on April 9, 2026, committing to a multiyear collaboration that underscores a fundamental shift in how the industry approaches AI infrastructure. Rather than betting exclusively on specialized accelerators, the two technology giants are doubling down on a balanced ecosystem combining traditional CPUs with custom infrastructure processors, signaling that general-purpose computing remains essential alongside specialized acceleration in modern AI systems.

sources.name: TechCrunch
SiliconANGLE
The Register
CNBC
Tom's Hardware
Intel Investor Relations

pull_quote.text: Scaling AI requires more than accelerators — it requires balanced systems. CPUs and IPUs are central to delivering the performance, efficiency and flexibility modern AI workloads demand.

stories.headline: TSMC Q1 2026 Revenue Jumps 35% to Record High as AI Chip Demand Holds Strong
stories.key_takeaway: TSMC's record Q1 2026 revenue of NT$1.134 trillion underscores the semiconductor industry's laser-focus on AI infrastructure, with Nvidia alone controlling the majority of the company's advanced packaging capacity and TSMC doubling down on U.S. expansion to meet insatiable global demand.
stories.article_excerpt: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company reported record Q1 2026 revenue of NT$1.134 trillion, up 35.1% year-over-year, driven by insatiable AI chip demand from Nvidia and Apple. Nvidia has secured over 60% of TSMC's advanced CoWoS packaging capacity through 2027, forcing TSMC to outsource overflow work and accelerate its $165 billion Arizona expansion to 12 planned fabs. The company expects 30-40% higher capex in 2026 as it races to close the gap between booming AI infrastructure demand and constrained semiconductor supply.

sources.name: InvestorIdeas.com
TechBuzz.ai
CNBC
Yahoo Finance
TrendForce
Tom's Hardware

pull_quote.text: The AI segment of TSMC's business pulled the weight.

data_points.label: Share of CoWoS capacity booked by Nvidia through 2027

stories.headline: Meta Unveils Muse Spark, First AI Model Built Under Alexandr Wang's Superintelligence Labs
stories.key_takeaway: Meta's release of Muse Spark marks a historic pivot from open-source to closed-model strategy, betting its $14.3 billion investment in Alexandr Wang's vision on proprietary AI that will power billions of users across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Ray-Ban glasses.
stories.article_excerpt: On April 8, Meta announced Muse Spark, the first flagship AI model from Alexandr Wang's newly formed superintelligence labs—a $14.3 billion bet on transforming the company's AI strategy. Unlike Meta's legacy of open-source Llama models, Muse Spark is proprietary and closed, available only through Meta's products. The model powers Meta's AI assistant across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Ray-Ban glasses, with capabilities in medical reasoning and general intelligence. Mark Zuckerberg stated the goal is to build AI agents that "do things for you," positioning this as part of Meta's broader vision for personal superintelligence. Meta projects $115-135 billion in AI capital spending for 2026, signaling an unprecedented commitment to AI dominance.

sources.name: TechCrunch
Fortune
Bloomberg
CNBC
Axios
CNN Business

pull_quote.text: We are building products that don't just answer your questions but act as agents that do things for you.

data_points.label: Meta's investment in Scale AI to acquire Alexandr Wang
Month Alexandr Wang joined Meta as Chief AI Officer

stories.headline: NVIDIA Releases Cosmos Open World Foundation Models to Accelerate Physical AI
stories.key_takeaway: NVIDIA's Cosmos open foundation models democratize physical AI development by providing developers with customizable, reasoning-capable models trained on internet-scale video and robotics data, enabling rapid advancement in robotics, autonomous vehicles, and industrial automation without requiring proprietary expertise.
stories.article_excerpt: NVIDIA has unleashed Cosmos, a family of open world foundation models designed to democratize physical AI development and bring humanlike spatial reasoning to robots, autonomous vehicles, and industrial systems. Announced on April 9, 2026, the three-model family—Cosmos Reason 2, Transfer 2.5, and Predict 2.5—enables organizations to fine-tune advanced capabilities for robotics, synthetic data generation, and autonomous systems without starting from scratch. With models available on Hugging Face, GitHub, and Google Cloud, NVIDIA democratizes access to state-of-the-art physical AI, potentially accelerating adoption across robotics and autonomous vehicle industries.

sources.name: NVIDIA Blog - Open Models, Data Tools to Accelerate AI
NVIDIA Newsroom - Major Release of Cosmos Foundation Models
TechCrunch - NVIDIA GTC 2026 Keynote Coverage

pull_quote.text: Cosmos introduces an open and fully customizable reasoning model for physical AI and unlocks opportunities for step-function advances in robotics and the physical industries.

data_points.label: Core Cosmos model families (Reason, Transfer, Predict)

stories.headline: Anthropic Launches Claude Managed Agents API to Run Enterprise AI Workflows at Scale
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic has eliminated the operational complexity of building enterprise AI agents by offering a fully managed cloud infrastructure service that handles sandboxing, state management, and execution oversight—allowing developers to deploy production agents in weeks instead of months.
stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic has launched Claude Managed Agents, a cloud-hosted infrastructure service that simplifies enterprise AI agent deployment in public beta as of April 8, 2026. The service eliminates months of engineering work by handling sandboxing, state management, credential security, and execution tracing—allowing developers to deploy production agents in weeks instead of months at a cost of $0.08 per active session hour. Early adopters including Notion, Rakuten, Sentry, and Asana have already reported dramatic productivity gains, with organizations deploying enterprise-wide agents within days. The platform's comprehensive feature set, combined with its managed infrastructure approach, positions Claude Managed Agents as a potential standard for enterprise AI agent deployment.

sources.name: Anthropic Engineering Blog
Claude Blog
The New Stack
SiliconANGLE
Help Net Security
InfoWorld

pull_quote.text: We want Notion to be the best place for teams to work with agents and get things done.

data_points.label: Cost per session hour (on top of standard token pricing)

stories.key_takeaway: Z.ai's GLM-5.1 becomes the first Chinese open-source model to top the global SWE-Bench Pro leaderboard, signaling China's expanding competitive advantage in specialized AI coding models.
stories.article_excerpt: Z.ai released GLM-5.1, a 754-billion parameter open-source coding model that topped the SWE-Bench Pro leaderboard with a score of 58.4, surpassing OpenAI's GPT-5.4 and Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6.

stories.headline: DrugCLIP: AI Framework Achieves 10-Million-Fold Speedup in Virtual Drug Screening
stories.key_takeaway: A new contrastive learning framework called DrugCLIP can screen billions of drug compounds against protein targets in hours instead of years, potentially transforming how medicines are discovered.
stories.article_excerpt: Researchers unveiled DrugCLIP, an AI framework that screens drug molecules 10 million times faster than traditional methods, evaluating 10 trillion protein-drug combinations in 24 hours.

pull_quote.text: This enables cross-screening campaigns involving over 10 trillion protein-molecule pairs.

stories.headline: MIT's AI-Powered Lab Discovers Record-Breaking Fuel Cell Catalyst in Just 90 Days
stories.key_takeaway: MIT researchers demonstrated that AI-guided robotic systems can accelerate materials discovery by orders of magnitude, finding a fuel cell catalyst 9.3 times more powerful than industry standard in less than three months.
stories.article_excerpt: MIT's CRESt platform, an AI-powered lab that combines LLMs with automated robotics, discovered a fuel cell catalyst with 9.3x the power density of the industry standard in just 90 days.

sources.name: AI system learns from many types of scientific information and runs experiments to discover new materials

stories.article_excerpt: Microsoft released Harrier-OSS-v1, an open-source multilingual embedding model family supporting 100+ languages and achieving state-of-the-art performance on MTEB v2.

stories.headline: xAI Sues Colorado Over Nation's First Comprehensive AI Bias Law, Citing First Amendment
stories.key_takeaway: xAI's lawsuit escalates the constitutional battle over state AI regulation, testing whether comprehensive bias safeguards can survive First Amendment and Commerce Clause scrutiny.
stories.article_excerpt: Elon Musk's xAI filed suit against Colorado on April 9, challenging SB 24-205, the nation's first comprehensive state AI bias law, arguing it violates the First Amendment.

pull_quote.text: The law prohibits developers of AI systems from producing speech that the State of Colorado dislikes, while compelling them to conform their speech to a State-enforced orthodoxy on controversial topics of great public concern.

stories.headline: South Korea's Basic AI Act Takes Extraterritorial Effect, Reshaping Global AI Compliance
stories.key_takeaway: South Korea's AI Basic Act enforcement now applies extraterritorially to foreign AI companies serving Korean users, creating compliance burdens alongside opportunities for local tech giants.
stories.article_excerpt: South Korea's AI Basic Act enforcement imposes extraterritorial compliance obligations on foreign AI companies serving Korean users, with risk-based requirements and watermarking of synthetic content.

stories.headline: Maine Passes Strict Limits on AI in Licensed Therapy After Wave of Teen Harm Incidents
stories.key_takeaway: Maine LD 2082 restricts how licensed therapists use AI while direct-to-consumer AI chatbots like Character.AI and Replika face mounting regulation across states.
stories.article_excerpt: Maine's legislature approved LD 2082, restricting how licensed therapists use AI in clinical settings, as states move to regulate AI mental health tools in the wake of teen suicides linked to Character.AI and Replika.
